backlog.title: Compact read-tool-style display via custom-message delivery
backlog.description:
Swap the file delivery model from appending <file> blocks into the user prompt text (

---

 separator) to delivering them as a single custom message returned from a before_agent_start handler, rendered compactly in the chat as green collapsible read <path> lines via a registered MessageRenderer. The model still receives the same <file> blocks (byte-identical content); only the message boundary and chat display change. All injection engine logic (regex, resolution, classification, paging, budget, markdown imports, dedup, config, autocomplete) is untouched.

milestones.title: Implement delivery + renderer in file-injector.ts
milestones.description: Thread FileDetail metadata through the injection core, split delivery across input + before_agent_start hooks with a one-shot stash, register a green read-tool-style MessageRenderer, and expose blocks/details from injectFiles. The existing file-injector.ts (1114 lines) ships the OLD append-to-prompt model and must be updated in place.

tasks.title: Thread FileDetail metadata through the injection core
tasks.description:
Add the FileDetail interface and a details: FileDetail[] field to State; make emitText push text/paged details alongside blocks; make the image and binary branches in injectFile push their details; and change injectFiles' return to expose blocks + details with a stripped-only text (delete the 

---

 concatenation). This is the data-structure foundation that the renderer and custom-message delivery consume.

subtasks.title: Add FileDetail interface, State.details field, and emitText detail pushes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current State interface at file-injector.ts L329-336 has fields {blocks, images, injectedSet, remaining, count, paged, bareAt} — it has NO details field. The emitText function at L752-792 pushes ONLY to state.blocks (whole: formatTextFileBlock; paged: formatTextFileBlock(head) + formatPagedDirectiveBlock). It never pushes any metadata/detail object. There is no FileDetail type anywhere in the codebase.
2. INPUT: The existing State interface (L329-336) and emitText function (L752-792) from the current file-injector.ts. The existing format helpers (formatTextFileBlock, formatPagedDirectiveBlock, headSlice, headCompleteLineCount).
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §6.2 FileDetail and §6.4 shared state. (a) Add interface FileDetail at module level near the State interface: { path: string; kind: 'text' | 'image' | 'binary' | 'paged'; chars?: number; lines?: number; range?: string; pagedHeadLines?: number; dimensionHint?: string }. (b) Add details: FileDetail[] to the State interface (parallel to blocks). (c) In emitText (L752-792): for the WHOLE path (state.remaining === null || fileCost <= PAGED_THRESHOLD * state.remaining), after state.blocks.push(formatTextFileBlock(abs, content)), also state.details.push({ path: abs, kind: 'text', chars: content.length, lines: lineCount }). For the SUB-HEAD guard branch (content.length <= HEAD_CHARS), same whole detail. For the PAGED branch, after pushing head block + directive block, state.details.push({ path: abs, kind: 'paged', chars: content.length, range: `:${startLine}-`, pagedHeadLines: headLines }). The lineCount variable already exists in emitText as (content.match(/\n/g)?.length ?? 0) + 1.
4. OUTPUT: An updated file-injector.ts where State has a details field, emitText pushes FileDetail entries alongside block pushes, and the FileDetail interface is exported (it will be consumed by P1.M1.T1.S2 for image/binary details and by P1.M1.T2.S2 for the renderer). NOTE: the State constructor in injectFiles (L941-948) must also initialize details: [] when creating the state object.
5. DOCS: none — internal type/interface additions; no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Add image/binary detail pushes and change injectFiles return shape (strip the --- concat)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The injectFile function at L689-736 has three relevant branches: IMAGE (L711-720) pushes to state.images + state.blocks (formatImageBlock) but NO detail; BINARY (L728-731) pushes to state.blocks (formatBinaryBlock) but NO detail. The injectFiles function at L906-988 returns Promise<{ text: string; images: ImageContent[]; injected: number; paged: number }>. At L987 it concatenates: const finalText = `${strippedText}

---

${state.blocks.join('

')}`. The string 

---

 appears ONLY at L987 and must be deleted from the output path.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1's updated State with details field and emitText detail pushes. The FileDetail interface. The current injectFile image/binary branches (L689-736) and injectFiles return (L906-988).
3. LOGIC: (a) IMAGE branch (after state.blocks.push(formatImageBlock(abs, resized))): state.details.push({ path: abs, kind: 'image', dimensionHint: resized ? formatDimensionNote(resized) ?? undefined : undefined }). The formatDimensionNote import already exists. (b) BINARY branch (after state.blocks.push(formatBinaryBlock(abs))): state.details.push({ path: abs, kind: 'binary' }). (c) EMPTY IMAGE branch (L705-708, formatEmptyImageBlock): push { path: abs, kind: 'image', dimensionHint: undefined } to keep details parallel with blocks. (d) CHANGE injectFiles return type (L906) to Promise<{ text: string; images: ImageContent[]; injected: number; paged: number; blocks: string[]; details: FileDetail[] }>. (e) DELETE the finalText concatenation at L987 — replace with: return { text: strippedText, images: state.images, injected: state.count, paged: state.paged, blocks: state.blocks, details: state.details }. The early-return at L971 (count===0) stays unchanged (returns original text/images, injected:0, paged:0, blocks:[], details:[]) — add blocks:[] and details:[] to it for shape consistency.
4. OUTPUT: injectFiles returns a 6-field object { text(stripped-only), images, injected, paged, blocks, details }. The string 

---

 no longer appears in the output path. Image/binary/empty-image branches push FileDetail entries. This is the seam consumed by P1.M1.T2.S1 (the input handler reads blocks/details to stash them).
5. DOCS: none — internal function signature change; no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Split delivery across two hooks + register the MessageRenderer
tasks.description: Add a pending closure stash; change the input handler to stash {blocks, details} and return the stripped prompt; add a before_agent_start handler that consumes the stash and returns the custom message; register a MessageRenderer for fileInjector.injected that draws a green read-tool-style box; add the new imports (highlightCode, getLanguageFromPath, Box, Text, Component).

subtasks.title: input/before_agent_start split with one-shot pending stash
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The factory function (export default function(pi: ExtensionAPI)) at L1028 currently registers: session_start (config load, L1030), input (L1032), session_start (autocomplete, L1060). There is NO before_agent_start handler and NO pending stash anywhere. The input handler at L1032-1053 calls injectFiles and returns { action: 'transform', text, images } where text contains the OLD-model appended blocks. The config variable `cfg` is module-level (L1024) NOT a closure variable. The Verified API (architecture/external_deps.md): before_agent_start event exists on ExtensionAPI.on(); BeforeAgentStartEventResult.message? is Pick<CustomMessage, 'customType'|'content'|'display'|'details'>; returning undefined is a no-op.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S2's injectFiles returning { text(stripped), images, injected, paged, blocks, details }. The current factory at L1028+ with its input handler at L1032-1053.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §3.4 two-mechanism model, §6.2 delivery, §6.4 two returns, §12.20 two-hook stash. (a) Add a closure variable inside the factory: let pending: { blocks: string[]; details: FileDetail[] } | null = null. NOTE: cfg is module-level (keep it that way — the test harness calls the factory fresh per captureHandler). (b) MODIFY the input handler (L1032): after calling injectFiles and checking injected > 0, set pending = { blocks, details } (from the injectFiles return), and return { action: 'transform', text: strippedText (NO blocks), images }. The text is now just the stripped prompt. (c) ADD pi.on('before_agent_start', async (_e, _ctx) => { if (!pending) return undefined; const { blocks, details } = pending; pending = null; return { message: { customType: 'fileInjector.injected', content: blocks.join('\n\n'), display: true, details: { files: details } } }; }). Clear pending unconditionally (one-shot per prompt) per PRD §12.20. (d) Register this handler AFTER the input handler registration (order within the factory does not matter for runtime since Pi dispatches by event name, but placing it after input is conventional).
4. OUTPUT: The factory registers three+ handlers: session_start(config), input (stashes pending, returns stripped text), before_agent_start (consumes pending → custom message or undefined), session_start(autocomplete). The pending stash is the handoff between input and before_agent_start. Consumed by P1.M1.T2.S2 which adds the renderer registration alongside.
5. DOCS: none — internal handler wiring; no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Register MessageRenderer + implement renderInjectedMessage with green read-tool-style display
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current file has ZERO imports from @earendil-works/pi-tui (L1-6 imports only from pi-coding-agent, pi-ai, and node builtins). Verified API (architecture/external_deps.md): (a) registerMessageRenderer<T>(customType, renderer) exists; MessageRenderer<T> = (message: CustomMessage<T>, options: {expanded:boolean}, theme: Theme) => Component | undefined. (b) CRITICAL NUANCE: toolSuccessBg is ThemeBg → use theme.bg('toolSuccessBg', text); toolTitle/accent/dim/toolOutput/warning are ThemeColor → use theme.fg(...). Using theme.fg('toolSuccessBg', ...) is a TYPE ERROR. (c) Box constructor: new Box(paddingX?, paddingY?, bgFn?). Text constructor: new Text(text?, paddingX?, paddingY?, customBgFn?). Box applies bgFn to ALL children. (d) highlightCode(code: string, lang?: string): string[] and getLanguageFromPath(filePath: string): string | undefined are both exported from pi-coding-agent. (e) Component is an interface (render(width): string[]) exported from pi-tui.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1's FileDetail interface. P1.M1.T2.S1's factory with before_agent_start handler. The constants and helpers already in the file (formatTextFileBlock, etc.).
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §6.3 chat display, §12.23-25 gotchas. (a) ADD IMPORTS at L1-6: add highlightCode, getLanguageFromPath to the pi-coding-agent import (alongside resizeImage, formatDimensionNote, CONFIG_DIR_NAME, getAgentDir, type ResizedImage). Add a NEW import line: import { Box, Text, type Component } from '@earendil-works/pi-tui'. (b) REGISTER RENDERER: in the FIRST session_start handler (L1030, the one that loads cfg), add after readConfig: pi.registerMessageRenderer('fileInjector.injected', (message, opts, theme) => renderInjectedMessage(message, opts, theme)). No hasUI guard needed — renderers are no-ops in print/json modes. (c) IMPLEMENT renderInjectedMessage(message, opts: {expanded}, theme): Component — at module level, after the existing format helpers. Parse files = message?.details?.files ?? []. Parse bodies from message.content via regex /<file name="([^"]+)">([\s\S]*?)<\/file>/g (re-derive content from the content string, not from details). Create const box = new Box(1, 1, (t: string) => theme.bg('toolSuccessBg', t)). If files.length === 0: defensive fallback — addChild(new Text(theme.fg('toolTitle', theme.bold('read')) + ' ' + theme.fg('dim', '(injected files)') + expandHint(theme), 0, 0)); if opts.expanded and message.content is a string, addChild(new Text(theme.fg('toolOutput', message.content), 0, 0)); return box. For each file d at index i: addChild(new Text(readLine(d, theme) + (i === 0 ? expandHint(theme) : ''), 0, 0)). If opts.expanded and bodies[i] !== undefined and d.kind !== 'image': const lang = d.kind === 'binary' ? undefined : getLanguageFromPath(d.path); const rendered = lang ? highlightCode(bodies[i], lang).join('\n') : bodies[i]; addChild(new Text(theme.fg('toolOutput', rendered), 0, 0)). Return box. (d) IMPLEMENT readLine(d: FileDetail, theme): string — title = theme.fg('toolTitle', theme.bold('read')); pathPart = theme.fg('accent', tildify(d.path)). If kind === 'binary': return title + ' ' + pathPart + ' ' + theme.fg('dim', '(binary — not injected)'). If kind === 'image': return title + ' ' + pathPart + (d.dimensionHint ? ' ' + theme.fg('dim', d.dimensionHint) : ''). If kind === 'paged': return title + ' ' + pathPart + theme.fg('warning', d.range ?? ''). Else (text): return title + ' ' + pathPart. (e) IMPLEMENT expandHint(theme): string — return ' ' + theme.fg('dim', '(ctrl+o to expand)'). (f) IMPLEMENT tildify(abs: string): string — const home = os.homedir(); return home && abs.startsWith(home + '/') ? '~' + abs.slice(home.length) : abs. (g) Export renderInjectedMessage so tests can call it directly.
4. OUTPUT: A registered MessageRenderer that produces a green Box with one read <tildified-path> line per file, expandable to full/highlighted content, defensive against missing details. Consumed by P1.M2.T2.S2 (renderer tests).
5. DOCS: none — internal renderer implementation. The user-facing display description is Mode B (README, handled by P1.M2.T3.S1).

milestones.title: Migrate tests, add delivery/display tests, and sync README (Mode B)
milestones.description: Migrate the ~170 existing test assertions from reading r.text for block content to reading r.blocks/r.details; add new tests for the custom-message delivery, stash lifecycle, and renderer output; and sync README.md to describe the new compact read-tool-style appearance (removing the --- append language).

tasks.title: Migrate existing test assertions to the new injectFiles return shape
tasks.description: Update all three test suites so assertions read r.blocks/r.details instead of r.text for file block content. The injection logic assertions (regex, cleanup, resolution, paging, imports, dedup, config) stay valid — only the container changes from text-embedded blocks to separate blocks/details arrays.

subtasks.title: Migrate file-injector.test.mjs (~170 assertions) to r.blocks/r.details
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: file-injector.test.mjs (2381 lines, ~122 cases) is a flat ESM script with runCase(n, name, fn) helpers. It calls mod.injectFiles(prompt, images, ctx, bareAt) and reads r.text. Approximately 170 assertions read r.text for: (a) the 

---

 separator (asserted explicitly ~2 times, e.g. L397/L415); (b) <file name="ABS">...</file> block content via r.text.includes() (~154 times); (c) ordering via r.text.indexOf() (~10 times); (d) <paged: directive presence (~4 times); (e) r.text === equality checks (~16 times for stripped prompt). The tests also capture input handlers via captureHandler('input') and read out.text/out.action. Under the NEW model out.text is stripped-only (no blocks). The test file also tests many exported pure functions (cleanToken, scanTokens, etc.) directly — those need NO migration.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S2's injectFiles returning { text(stripped), images, injected, paged, blocks: string[], details: FileDetail[] }. The completed implementation from P1.M1.T2.S2.
3. LOGIC: (a) DELETE all assertions of r.text.includes('

---

') — the separator no longer exists. (b) REPLACE r.text.includes('<file name="ABS">...') patterns with r.blocks.some(b => b.includes('<file name="ABS">')) or r.blocks.join('

').includes(...). (c) REPLACE ordering checks (r.text.indexOf) with index checks in r.blocks array (e.g. r.blocks.findIndex(b => b.includes('A')) < r.blocks.findIndex(b => b.includes('B'))). (d) REPLACE <paged: directive checks from r.text to r.blocks.some(b => b.includes('<paged:')). (e) ADD assertions that r.text === stripped prompt (no blocks, no ---). For handler-level tests (captureHandler): out.text from the input handler is now the stripped prompt; to verify blocks are delivered, capture before_agent_start too and assert the returned message.content contains them. (f) For the module-surface sanity section (~L97-149): add renderInjectedMessage to the expected exports. (g) The State literal in mock objects (e.g. L661) must add details: []. (h) Keep ALL injection logic assertions (regex, cleanup, resolution, paging, imports, dedup, config, bare-@, code-region detection) valid — their logic is unchanged, only the output container changed.
4. OUTPUT: file-injector.test.mjs passes with the new return shape. All ~170 migrated assertions read r.blocks/r.details instead of r.text. The suite prints the same pass/fail matrix.
5. DOCS: none — test file changes only; no user-facing surface.

subtasks.title: Migrate relative-imports.test.mjs and import-behavior.test.mjs return-shape reads
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: relative-imports.test.mjs (508 lines, ~38 cases) and import-behavior.test.mjs (250 lines, ~22 cases) both use a run(cwd, prompt, bareAt) wrapper that calls mod.injectFiles(prompt, [], ctxFor(cwd), bareAt). They read content via helper functions: has(text, marker) checks if a marker string appears in the text; blocksRel(text, root) extracts all <file name="ABS"> paths relative to root; countAbs(text, abs) counts occurrences. These helpers currently operate on out.text (which contained the blocks under the OLD model). Under the NEW model, blocks live in out.blocks (a string array). The import LOGIC tested (relative resolution, bare-@ at depth, trailing punctuation, exact-vs-shorthand) is UNCHANGED — only the output container moves.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S2's injectFiles returning { text(stripped), images, injected, paged, blocks: string[], details: FileDetail[] }. The completed implementation.
3. LOGIC: (a) In relative-imports.test.mjs: update the run() helper to return the full injectFiles result (including blocks/details). Update has(text, marker) to search r.blocks.join('

') instead of r.text — OR equivalently, change has() to take the result object and check result.blocks.join('

').includes(marker). Update blocksRel() and countAbs() to operate on r.blocks.join('

') instead of r.text. (b) In import-behavior.test.mjs: same pattern — update has(out, marker) to check out.blocks.join('

').includes(marker) instead of out.text.includes(marker). (c) For handler-level tests (captureHandlers/runViaHandler): out.text from input is stripped-only; to verify content delivery, capture before_agent_start and check message.content. (d) Keep ALL import logic assertions valid (file-relative resolution at every depth, bare-@ honoring, exact-beats-shorthand, code-region exemption, dedup, cycles).
4. OUTPUT: Both smaller suites pass with the new return shape. Import behavior regression coverage is preserved.
5. DOCS: none — test file changes only.

tasks.title: Add delivery, stash lifecycle, and renderer display tests
tasks.description: Add new runnable tests covering the NEW-model behaviors not exercised by the migrated suites: the injectFiles return shape (blocks/details arrays + stripped text), the before_agent_start custom-message emission and one-shot stash lifecycle, and the renderInjectedMessage output (green read-lines per file kind, defensive fallback).

subtasks.title: Delivery + custom-message + stash lifecycle tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The tests use captureHandler(event) to capture handlers: const pi = { on: (ev, cb) => { if (ev === event) cbs.push(cb); } }; mod.default(pi). To capture before_agent_start, the mock pi.on must also collect 'before_agent_start' callbacks. Currently captureHandler only captures one event name at a time. The cfg variable is module-level so it persists across factory calls.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T2.S1's factory with input + before_agent_start handlers and the pending stash. P1.M1.T1.S2's injectFiles returning {blocks, details}.
3. LOGIC: Add new test cases to file-injector.test.mjs (or a new section within it) covering PRD §11 test cases #33-41 + §10 display/delivery rows. (a) RETURN SHAPE: call injectFiles with a small file; assert r.blocks is an array of length 1 containing a <file name="..."> string; assert r.details is an array of length 1 with {path, kind:'text', chars, lines}; assert r.text === 'Review a.ts' (stripped, no blocks, no ---). (b) CUSTOM MESSAGE: capture both input and before_agent_start handlers; invoke input with a #@ prompt; invoke before_agent_start with the result; assert the returned message is { customType: 'fileInjector.injected', content: blocks.join('\n\n'), display: true, details: { files: [...] } }; assert message.content includes the <file> block. (c) ONE-SHOT STASH: invoke before_agent_start TWICE; assert the second returns undefined (pending was cleared). (d) EMPTY STASH: invoke input with a prompt containing NO #@; then invoke before_agent_start; assert it returns undefined (no phantom injection). (e) SHORT-CIRCUIT: invoke input with source:'extension'; then before_agent_start; assert undefined. (f) MULTI-FILE: injectFiles with two #@ tokens; assert r.blocks.length === 2 and r.details.length === 2 in emission order.
4. OUTPUT: New test cases in file-injector.test.mjs that pin the delivery model, stash lifecycle, and custom-message shape. These correspond to PRD §11 #33-41 (the display/model-input cases).
5. DOCS: none — test additions only.

subtasks.title: Renderer output + defensive fallback tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The renderer (renderInjectedMessage) is exported from the module and takes (message, opts:{expanded:boolean}, theme). It returns a Component (Box with children). The Box from pi-tui has a .children array of Component. Text has internal text (accessible via its constructor arg or by calling render()). A stub theme with fg/bg/bold that return their text arg lets the test inspect the unstyled string content. The FILE_BLOCK_RE regex in the renderer re-parses <file> blocks from message.content — so the test must provide a message with content matching that format.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T2.S2's exported renderInjectedMessage function. The FileDetail interface from P1.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Add new test cases covering PRD §6.3 and §12.23 (defensive renderer). Use a stub theme: const stubTheme = { fg: (k, t) => t, bg: (k, t) => t, bold: (t) => t }. (a) COLLAPSED SINGLE FILE: call renderInjectedMessage({ details: { files: [{ path: '/abs/a.ts', kind: 'text' }] }, content: '<file name="/abs/a.ts">\nhello\n</file>' }, { expanded: false }, stubTheme); assert the returned Box has children; the first child's rendered text includes 'read' and the path. (b) COLLAPSED MULTI-FILE: two files; assert two read lines + one expand hint. (c) PAGED LINE: kind:'paged' with range; assert the line includes the range suffix. (d) IMAGE LINE: kind:'image' with dimensionHint; assert the hint appears. (e) BINARY LINE: kind:'binary'; assert '(binary — not injected)'. (f) EXPANDED: {expanded:true}; assert more children (the file content renders below the read line). (g) IMAGE NOT RE-RENDERED: expanded + kind:'image'; assert no body child (images attach to user message, not renderer). (h) DEFENSIVE FALLBACK: call with { details: {} } (no files array) or { content: '...' } (no details); assert no throw, returns a Box with a single fallback '(injected files)' line. (i) TILDIFY: a path under os.homedir(); assert the display path starts with '~'.
4. OUTPUT: New test cases pinning renderer output for each file kind, expanded/collapsed behavior, and the defensive fallback. These are the PRD §11 #33-38 display test cases.
5. DOCS: none — test additions only.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)
tasks.description: Update README.md to describe the new compact read-tool-style chat display, removing all references to the removed --- append model. This is the cross-cutting changeset-level documentation sync that only makes sense once the full implementation (M1) and test migration (M2.T1-T2) are in place.

subtasks.title: Update README.md for compact read-tool-style display (remove --- append language)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md line 33-34 currently describes the OLD model verbatim: 'On submit, the file contents appear below a --- rule. The #@ trigger is stripped from each reference, so Review #@a.ts reaches the model as Review a.ts with the file appended underneath.' The 'What gets injected' section (line ~65) and the 'Syntax' section do not reference the --- model and mostly stay valid. The install/usage/syntax/limits sections are unaffected. The 'Text uses Pi native block format' code example (line ~78) showing <file name=...>...</file> is still correct (the model still receives that format).
2. INPUT: The completed implementation from P1.M1 (all subtasks). The migrated tests from P1.M2.T1 and new tests from P1.M2.T2 confirming the behavior. PRD §1 Solution (the 'How delivery works (and how it looks)' paragraph), §2 Goal #8, §6.3 chat display.
3. LOGIC: Rewrite the README 'Usage' section paragraph at line 33-34 to describe the NEW appearance. The OLD text 'On submit, the file contents appear below a --- rule. The #@ trigger is stripped...' must become something like: 'On submit, each file appears as a compact green read <path> line below your message — one per file, indistinguishable from the read tool. Press ctrl+o to expand the full contents. The #@ trigger is stripped from each reference, so Review #@a.ts shows as Review a.ts in your message, with the file delivered to the model underneath.' Also check: (a) the 'What gets injected' section — the <file name=...> code example is still correct for the model-facing format; keep it but add a note that in the chat the files appear as read lines (not as the raw block). (b) Remove any mention of '---' or 'appended underneath' anywhere in the README. (c) The tagline area at the top is unaffected ('the whole file, every time, everywhere' still holds). (d) Do NOT change install instructions, syntax rules, config docs, limits, or the #@ vs @ comparison — those are unchanged by this delta.
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately describes the compact read-tool-style display. No references to '---' append remain. The user-facing description matches what an end user sees in the TUI.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task. It depends on all implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1 through P1.M2.T2.S2) because it describes the complete delta's user-visible result.
